meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no design skills (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no design skills. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Solve Design Gaps for Freelancers (Guide)
content.seo_description: Overcome design limitations as a freelancer using AI-driven Git workflows. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to ship polished, professional-grade SaaS apps faster.
content.answer_block: Stop letting design gaps stall your production. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you can automate UI component generation directly from your repository. This workflow maps design requirements to clean, production-ready code, allowing you to bypass manual CSS/UI hurdles and ship high-fidelity interfaces at rapid, scalable speeds.
content.intro:
The blank canvas is a freelancer’s graveyard. You have the logic, the database schema, and the API endpoints, but the UI looks like a relic from 2004. Clients don't just pay for functionality; they pay for the aesthetic polish that transforms code into a premium product. 

We are entering an era where 'no design skills' is no longer a bottleneck. By weaponizing Git with AI, you transform your repository into a living design system. This isn't just about writing code; it’s about orchestrating an automated pipeline that turns your technical architecture into a high-converting user experience.

steps.title: Contextualize the Design System
steps.description: Establish your design language within the repository so the AI understands your constraints and brand identity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze existing Tailwind config and component library. Map out a consistent design system including spacing, typography, and color palette. Generate a design-tokens.json file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create a design-tokens.json file based on our existing project structure and Tailwind configuration.'

steps.title: Automate Component Generation
steps.description: Feed technical requirements into Claude Code to generate UI components that adhere to your established design system.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review current UI patterns. Create a new responsive dashboard card component that follows our design tokens and uses semantic HTML/Tailwind.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Generate a responsive dashboard card component using existing design tokens; ensure accessibility compliance.'

steps.title: Git-Driven Visual Regression
steps.description: Use Git branches to isolate UI experiments, allowing for safe iterations without breaking the production build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a branch for UI enhancement. Compare current component layout against industry best practices for SaaS dashboards.
steps.claude_code_command: git checkout -b feature/ui-refresh && claude run --instruction 'Refactor current dashboard layout to improve visual hierarchy.'

steps.title: Automated Code Review & Polish
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a final review of generated components to ensure they meet professional production standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the latest commit for UI inconsistencies, accessibility gaps, and performance bottlenecks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude review --path ./src/components --instruction 'Identify any non-standard CSS or accessibility violations.'

steps.title: Sync and Deploy
steps.description: Finalize the integration, update your documentation, and push the polished code to your main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate documentation for the new component and create a pull request summary.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'feat: add polished dashboard components' && git push origin feature/ui-refresh

pro_tips: Use a 'Design-Context.md' file in your root directory to keep the AI aligned with your specific style preferences across different sessions.
Always force the AI to use semantic HTML; it drastically improves both accessibility and SEO without requiring complex CSS overrides.
Run your components through a contrast-checker script after generation to ensure your AI-selected color palettes meet WCAG standards.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your UI components immediately after generation to ensure behavior consistency across screen sizes.
Chain your prompts: ask the AI to explain the design decisions it made, then use those explanations to refine your future design-tokens.json.
content.next_steps: Begin by auditing your current project's design consistency. Once you have a base design-tokens.json, start replacing one 'ugly' component per day using the Claude Code workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with CSS frameworks and start shipping high-fidelity SaaS products that command premium rates. Our community of elite builders is waiting to help you scale your output and master the AI-Git workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
